section: 4. How do we process your personal data?
text: How long will my data be saved? After a customer account is deactivated, the following retention periods apply: Without product usage: 3 years With product usage (invoicing): 14 years (in accordance with tax and commercial law retention obligations) Unverified customer accounts: 14 days after the end of the failed verification period Once these periods have expired, your data will be deleted.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text: The GDPR grants you the following rights regarding your data: Right of access: You can request information at any time about whether and which personal data we process about you.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text: Right to rectification: If the account information we have stored about you is incorrect or incomplete, you can request its correction or completion.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text: Right to erasure ("right to be forgotten"): You cannot request the deletion of your data solely on the grounds that it is no longer required for its original purpose.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text: However, please note that immediate deletion may not be possible in some cases due to legal or contractual retention obligations.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text: Right to restriction of processing: If, for example, you dispute the accuracy of your data or believe that the processing is unlawful, you must submit a written request by post to our data protection officer to restrict the processing of your data.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text: Right to object: You can object to the processing of your personal data unless there are compelling legitimate reasons for the processing.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text: Right not to be subject to automated decision-making: We do not make any automated decisions regarding your person.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text: If we did, you would have the right not to be evaluated solely by algorithms.

section: 6. Making complaints to the relevant data protection authority
text: Making complaints to the relevant data protection authority If you believe that we are violating any data protection laws by processing personal data, you also have the right to make a complaint to the data supervisory authority who is responsible for our company.
